title: Lepisorus obtusifrons
intro: Lepisorus obtusifrons is a species of fern, belonging to the genus Lepisorus, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Lepisorus obtusifrons include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lepisorus obtusifrons is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy:
Lepisorus obtusifrons is classified under the phylum Arthropoda, class Reptilia, order Squamata, family Geckonidae, and genus Lepisorus. 
This classification places Lepisorus obtusifrons within the broader group of geckos, which are primarily characterized by their ability to climb and adhere to surfaces. 
As a member of the family Geckonidae, Lepisorus obtusifrons shares similarities with other geckos in terms of its physical characteristics and behaviors. 
However, the specific classification of Lepisorus obtusifrons is more nuanced, with some sources potentially grouping it under a different family or subfamily. 
Further research or a more detailed taxonomic analysis may be necessary to determine the precise classification of Lepisorus obtusifrons.
